source.title: *NEW FOR 2025* How To Setup and Defend Bank ALL Sites - Rainbow Six Siege
source.file: BANK/_NEW FOR 2025_ How To Setup and Defend Bank ALL Sites - Rainbow Six Siege.txt
source.notes: Recorded on the CX/PTS test server ahead of the June 10 Bank rework. Generic ranked setups, not pro-league lineups. Room names below are the creator's informal callouts and need reconciling against data/maps/bank.json once wiki callouts are confirmed.

reinforcements.location: West Wall of CEO Office, Left Side (x2)
reinforcements.note: Shoot the top of the wall and C4 over it instead of a straight wall-bang.

reinforcements.location: Wall Between CEO Office and Janitor
reinforcements.note: Play Janitor tied to this door; hold into Stock through it.

reinforcements.location: East Wall of Kanto Wall Corridor, x2 (near Trump/Stock)
reinforcements.note: Bring a Bandit — the batteries are awkward for attackers to reach with EMP from this angle. Skip Thatcher, his EMP doesn't detonate on impact here.
Watch the west repel spot outside these windows.
Pair with a head hole into the same wall.

headHoles.location: West Wall of CEO Office, Right Side
headHoles.note: Keep this side soft — head holes onto Janitor only. Don't reinforce it, or Square opens up completely.

headHoles.location: Wall at Back of CEO Office (into Square)
headHoles.note: Best used with a DMR or ACOG.

headHoles.location: West Wall of CEO Office (facing the 4 windows)
headHoles.note: Don't make this the first thing you do in a round — play Janitor early instead.
Same wall as the reinforcement — gives sightline into Stock.

rotates.note: Make the rotate hole on the LEFT side, not the right — right side exposes you to the top of the spiral/banana stairs.

gadgetPlacements.note: Shoot the ceiling and place a camera covering both sides of the corridor into Stock.
Assume attackers can C4/frag through the floor from the open area below.

gadgetPlacements.location: West side of Meeting Room (desks room)
gadgetPlacements.note: Frost mats under each window; play behind the desks (clear the monitor first for sightline). Shield covers the angle toward Square. Watch for grenades thrown in — bring Kaid wall or Jäger if you can.

operatorRecommendations.note: Runs this site most rounds; sets up the corridor camera.
Runs the Meeting Room desks room with mats/Kiba barriers on the window angles.

generalTips.note: Don't reinforce every CEO/window-facing wall — leave at least one angle to shoot back from.
Fire extinguishers can be popped to dash/stun-and-run past a defender holding a doorway (Janitor, or back of CEO past the two windows).

reinforcements.note: Only reinforce if dedicating a player to permanently hold Server. Otherwise leave it soft.
Always reinforce this one.
Optional — make feet holes here instead if you'd rather see feet at the back of Server from the rotate spot.

reinforcements.location: Wall near Red Hall default plant
reinforcements.note: Prefer head holes over reinforcing here.
Either reinforce it fully or make head holes — don't do both, and don't head-hole the entire wall.

headHoles.note: Two head holes covering the default plant from different angles, plus a wider DMR swing that also covers the side door.
Pair with a DMR-capable defender (e.g. Aruni) or ACOG loadout.
Higher than a normal head hole — smokes thrown from deep Lockers can reach the Server door/plant spot; a C4 lobbed from close also lands roughly on the plant.

rotates.note: Make the rotate on the LEFT side, not the right. Use the bomb chassis as cover while moving through.

hatches.note: Reinforced. Don't assume one smoke clears the room — it's big.
Leave open. Smoke is effective at flushing out attackers who drop in.

gadgetPlacements.location: Dirt (thrown in, not from the doorway)
gadgetPlacements.note: Prox mine thrown into Dirt for an early warning.

gadgetPlacements.location: Top of stairs behind the sofa
gadgetPlacements.note: Second prox mine placement — covers the Dirt and Blue approaches together.

gadgetPlacements.location: Behind the bomb, facing the hatch
gadgetPlacements.note: Crouch and throw the electroclaw from directly behind the bomb, not from an exposed angle. Stand up to retrieve it if needed.

operatorRecommendations.note: Reinforces and plays the Server hatch; runs both prox mines.
Keeps the hatch near the bomb closed with electroclaw from a safe crouch angle.
Covers the Garage push, default plant, and side door through the Garage head holes.

generalTips.note: Gold is mainly a safe rotate path toward the hatch-drop area.
Red Hall's sprinkler/fireball hazard has no counter — no breakable glass panels nearby.
A defender upstairs at this site should actively cause delays, not sit passive — retreat down Main Stairs by shooting out steps and crouch-running down.
If the wall near Vault gets opened, attackers can crouch in with their back protected by the wall — only specific head-hole angles see the plant spot directly.

reinforcements.note: Pair with feet holes along the wall.
Optional. Reinforcing it means leaving at least one basement hatch soft instead.

reinforcements.location: Both sides of the Small Office to Talis rotate
reinforcements.note: Reinforce both sides. Middle placement preferred — also lets you hold toward the Talis door.

reinforcements.location: Wall near Small Office (extra, budget permitting)
reinforcements.note: Extra protection against people shooting up through the wall from Main Stairs.
Pair with a rotate/head holes there.

headHoles.location: Small Office, watching the Open Area feet holes
headHoles.note: Watch the feet holes from cover instead of standing exposed in the open.

rotates.location: Small Office → Talis (middle of wall preferred)

rotates.note: Moves you between the two halves of the site without looping via Kitchen door.

hatches.location: Both hatches above this site (Stock hatch + Janitor-side hatch)
hatches.note: Leave soft/open. If forced to reinforce one, pick the Stock hatch.

gadgetPlacements.note: Feet holes for watching jump-ins; pair with proxy mines or a trap gadget.
Hold Stock from above behind a shield (Clash) or Welcome Mats covering the approach (Frost); fall back through the hatch once pressure builds.
Leave the Janitor-side hatch open, add a small rotate nearby. Don't use this rotate in roughly the first 90 seconds — attackers often camp it early. Place goo mines on the rotate approach.
Alternative pick — Prisma projectors near the rotate.

operatorRecommendations.note: Holds the Janitor-adjacent pocket above this site with trap/intel gadgets and a rotate back to CEO.
Holds Kitchen with a shotgun once the wall there gets opened.

generalTips.note: Only vert here is the Stock Room footprint directly above Kitchen (visible through a ceiling hole).
If holding Stock upstairs, reinforce the wall facing the Square-equivalent room.
Never peek the long Back Open Area window toward the street/cars.
Watch for a prone punch-hole angle attackers make on the left wall of Lone — long sightline across Open Area to Kitchen door.
Always fire your weapon while dropping through a hatch to mask the drop noise as a gunshot.
Kitchen chairs are wall-bangable (not breakable by melee/punching).
Roughly 14 useful reinforcement spots exist across this site, but only 10 total are available for the whole map — prioritize.

reinforcements.note: Same pattern as the CEO Office/Meeting Room site: the two key walls, head-hole-into-Square-equivalent combo, and head holes into the Stock-equivalent room.
Optional — shrinks the area you have to defend.
Reinforce both.
Stops attackers at the bottom of Main Stairs shooting straight through into Lobby.

reinforcements.location: Optional extra wall for Open Area (upper)
reinforcements.note: Makes holding the upper Open Area equivalent room easier from below.

headHoles.note: Shoot the top off the plant on the shelf, tuck behind it, then shoot out a stack of paper to open a sightline down Electrical.

rotates.location: Same rotate structure as CEO/Meeting site
Rotate on one end of a specific Lobby-adjacent wall
rotates.note: Use carefully — looks straight into Main Lobby, West repels are a risk here.

hatches.note: Keep open, not reinforced. Don't drop it carelessly early — attackers commonly push the upper Open Area room through its window.

gadgetPlacements.note: Same shield + mats setup as the CEO/Meeting site's desks room.

operatorRecommendations.operator: Ela, Lesion, or another trap-style defender (e.g. Kapkan, Melusi)
operatorRecommendations.note: Covers both downstairs side approaches with up to three retreat routes back toward Main Lobby.
Usable, but a well-known angle to good attacking teams.

generalTips.note: Recommended 3-player upstairs split: one on Front Desk, one in Talis, one in the upper Open Area room.
Front Desk needs at least light coverage.
If defense loses the upstairs fight, this site becomes very weak — everything upstairs is soft with verticality everywhere.
Considered the weakest site on the map — prefer Open Area/Kitchen if you have the choice.
